text.title: Two-Pass Generation and Drift Detection
text.summary: Two-pass pipeline for agent-generated docs: discovery → write → verify, plus nightly drift checks.

section.title: Generation Pipeline

rule.statement: Generate AGENTS.md in two passes: Pass 1 discovers actual files via git ls-files; Pass 2 writes from the outline only — no invention.
rule.rationale: Single-pass generation produces hallucinated file names and invented exports. Separating discovery from writing forces the writer to use only verified inputs.

rule.statement: Pass 3 (verification): re-read the produced doc, open every file/path mentioned, report broken references before committing.
rule.rationale: Broken references in AGENTS.md mislead subsequent agents. Automated verification is cheaper than manual review of every generated doc.

section.reference: Discovery script: emits JSON outline from git ls-files + tokei

example.code: Create AGENTS.md for <dir>. Strict rules: - Length 20-80 lines. - Every entry in the file table must correspond to an actual file (verified by Read). - No ASCII art. Tables, lists, arrows (->), directory trees only. - English only. - If a section has nothing real to say, omit it. Input outline (JSON): <outline> Output: contents of AGENTS.md only, no commentary.
example.note: Pass JSON outline as the only source of file truth. The writer prompt must not be allowed to add files it "knows about" from training data.

example.code: Verify <path>/AGENTS.md against the actual directory. For every file/symbol mentioned, Read or grep to confirm existence. Output JSON: { "missing_refs": [...], "stale_tables": [...] }.
example.note: Verification prompt. Run after every write; block commit if missing_refs is non-empty.

rule.statement: Run a doc-drift check on every PR that touches a module's source files. A nightly job is a fallback, not the primary path.
rule.rationale: Docs committed in the same PR as code changes are accurate at merge. Docs updated only nightly drift within hours of a refactor.

rule.statement: Add a "Last verified: commit-hash" footer to generated docs. Mark stale in CI if the hash is more than N commits behind HEAD.
rule.rationale: Makes staleness machine-detectable. Agents can skip reading docs flagged as stale.
